headline_subtitle: Solid-state biology, tumor physics, geomagnetic noise. No fluff.

cards.body: Kruse reframes teeth as crystalline {{concept:Light-Pipes}} and mineral-dielectric capacitors derived from the same embryonic ectoderm that forms the brain, neural crest, and retina. The implication: dental mineralization status is a direct readout of the same field that runs CNS coherence — not a downstream cosmetic concern.

Light-Pipes.text: Solid-state crystalline structures that conduct photons through their mineral lattice with low scattering. Kruse's framing treats enamel and dentin as a coupled optical waveguide tied to the retinal-pineal axis via shared ectodermal origin — meaning oral mineral state reflects systemic photonic coupling.

cards.source_quote: Teeth are not passive chewing stones; they are highly specialized, solid-state crystalline light-pipes and mineral-dielectric capacitors derived directly from the embryonic ectoderm; the identical cellular lineage that wraps and forms the brain, the neural crest, and the retina.

cards.body: Direct rebuttal to the {{concept:Seyfried}}-line claim that cancer cells can't process fats. Kruse argues tumor adaptation lets cells run on fats once glucose is cut 100% — the {{concept:18F-FDG}} PET imaging story is a measurement artifact, not a metabolic ceiling. Sharp warning that 'starve the tumor with keto' messaging is dangerous to advanced patients who skip real treatment.

Seyfried.text: Thomas Seyfried — Boston College biologist, lead proponent of the 'cancer as a metabolic disease' framework. Best known for the ketogenic-diet-starves-tumor thesis built on Otto Warburg's glucose-dependence observation. Kruse argues this oversimplifies tumor metabolic plasticity.

18F-FDG.text: Radioactive glucose analogue used as PET imaging contrast. Lights up high-glucose-uptake tissue (most active tumors) — often misread as proof tumors only consume glucose. In reality it reflects what they preferentially consume under fed conditions, not what they're capable of metabolizing under glucose restriction.

cards.source_quote: The assertion that cancer cells cannot process fats is flatly disproven by the physics of tumor adaptation.

cards.body: A rare ring-cloud 'falling hole' opened over Kansas, Nebraska, Iowa and the Dakotas at sunrise on 19 May. Kruse links the event to {{concept:Telluric Currents}}, the local geomagnetic field state, and recent shifts in the {{concept:Schumann Resonance}} — another data point in the dipole-decline narrative.

Telluric Currents.text: Slow-moving electric currents that flow through Earth's crust and oceans, driven by the geomagnetic field and solar-wind interactions. Anomalies correlate with dipole instability.

Schumann Resonance.text: The ~7.83 Hz electromagnetic standing wave in the cavity between Earth's surface and the ionosphere. Shifts in its frequency or amplitude track changes in the planet's electromagnetic envelope.

cards.source_quote: Given the Telluric currents and the state of Kansas magnetic field and the recent changes to the Schumann resonance, Makes decentralized sense.

bullets.title: Tooth Loss ↔ Glioma — Shared Ectoderm Lineage
bullets.summary: New hypothesis: dental mineralisation defects and glioma incidence share the same embryonic {{concept:ectoderm}} origin — dentin matrix and CNS glia descend from one cell line, so a single field failure can explain both. Direct extension of today's teeth-as-light-pipes tweet.

ectoderm.text: The outermost of the three primary germ layers in early embryonic development. It gives rise to the nervous system, skin, hair, teeth enamel and the retina — meaning the brain and the teeth derive from the same starter cells.

bullets.title: De-Fragging the Water Table with LSD
bullets.summary: Novel protocol angle: psychedelic-induced dopamine surge as a temporal-redox lever for {{concept:de-fragging}} the intracellular water lattice. First time an exogenous compound is explicitly tied to the de-fragging protocol — speculative, but a concrete mechanism.

de-fragging.text: Kruse-coined term for restoring structured (EZ) water lattice coherence in the cell, reversing the disordered state that sets in under deuterium load + nnEMF exposure. Analogous to defragmenting a fragmented hard disk, but for the intracellular water phase.

bullets.title: Brachistochrone of Decentralized Biology
bullets.summary: Mapping the {{concept:Brachistochrone}} curve onto the optimal path through a redox gradient — fastest route from low- to high-coherence cellular state. Frames protocol stacking as a path-optimization problem, not a flat checklist.

Brachistochrone.text: Classical mechanics problem (Johann Bernoulli, 1696): the curve along which a falling object reaches a destination point in the shortest time under gravity — turns out to be a cycloid, not a straight line. Kruse uses it as an analogy: the fastest path through a redox gradient is not a straight stack of habits but a specifically-shaped sequence.

bullets.title: 3 Melanins — Upstream Cadaver-Cohort Citations
bullets.summary: Dr Rob surfaces the {{concept:Teghan Lucas}} / {{concept:Maciej Henneberg}} cadaver-cohort work as the actual primary source for the cranial-secular-trend literature across industrialised northern-European populations — the data centralized takes typically misread.

Teghan Lucas.text: Australian anatomist (Flinders University). Known for cadaver-based studies documenting modern secular trends in human skeletal anatomy — e.g., the rise of the external occipital protuberance ('phone horn') in younger populations.

Maciej Henneberg.text: Polish-Australian biological anthropologist (University of Adelaide), long-time Lucas collaborator. Builds population-scale anatomical datasets that document microevolution and secular trends across industrialised societies.

bullets.title: nnEMF Field-Survey Protocol
bullets.summary: Concrete home-shielding workflow: walk the perimeter to identify the strongest-signal side, scan for nearby cell towers; if no tower is close, attribute to neighbour Wi-Fi. For single-story homes, wire-mesh fence shielding on the implicated side. Transferable, parameter-light.

bullets.title: Hypermobility & Ehlers-Danlos as Field-Coupling Failure
bullets.summary: Mapping connective-tissue laxity in Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome to localized loss of melanin-tied redox shielding — extends the coherence thesis to a defined clinical phenotype. Implies the protocol stack for hypermobile patients should center on redox restoration, not collagen support.
